title: FGAP-130 citation-rot regression test is naive-text-scan-based with heuristic exclusions deferring rigor — mandate-007 violation in plan text 'is sufficient — pick complexity later if false-positives surface'; AST-based traversal foreclosing both false-positive and false-negative classes never built
description: FGAP-130 plan step 3 + implementation landed a regression test in packages/pi-context/src/samples-catalog.test.ts that: (a) does naive recursive JSON walk for samples/**/*.json — every string value; (b) does line-by-line raw text scan of src/index.ts + src/orientation.ts; (c) uses heuristic exclusions ('property named id at item level' for JSON, 'comment-only line OR line containing both e.g. and NNN' for source). The plan text explicitly deferred rigor with the phrase 'pick complexity later only if false-positives surface' — a mandate-007 anti-pattern (don't favor deferring discovered issues; if a known-fragility-possibility exists, surface and foreclose it now). Specific known fragility classes the naive scan misses or risks: (1) MULTI-LINE STRINGS in TypeScript — template literals or split-across-newlines string concatenation where a canonical_id straddles the line break escapes the line-by-line scan; (2) COMMENT-EXCLUSION FALSE NEGATIVE — line with code + trailing inline comment containing a citation (`const x = 'y'; // see TASK-088`) is NOT excluded but the citation is dev-internal; not currently a leak path but the exclusion logic isn't doing what its phrasing implies; (3) e.g.+NNN HEURISTIC FALSE POSITIVE — a legitimate sentence containing 'e.g.' and the digits 'NNN' in unrelated contexts collides with the exclusion; conversely, a real citation in a sentence that happens to contain both substrings escapes detection; (4) JSON enum-value FALSE POSITIVE — a schema enum like ['FGAP-001', 'FGAP-002'] would trip the test even though enums of canonical_ids may be legitimate substrate-vocabulary declarations (none ship today but could land); (5) STRUCTURAL ID EXCLUSION is path-suffix-based ('samples/blocks/*.json') and key-name-based ('property named id') — does not distinguish item-level id from nested id (e.g. an object {someField: {id: 'TASK-001'}} nested in a description-context); (6) JSDoc-comment dev-internal IDs in TypeScript are not scanned at all (current scope) but the line-by-line exclusion is unaware whether a string literal IS in JSDoc context vs in a tool-description string — if a JSDoc-style block contains a string literal with a citation, it's still in source text and the test may flag or miss inconsistently. The AST-based alternative: parse TS via tsc/typescript compiler API, identify all string-literal nodes inside pi.registerTool({description: ...}) calls + parameter description fields specifically, and scan only those — eliminates JSDoc + comment confusion entirely. Parse JSON via deep visitor with full JSONPath tracking + carve out exact paths (e.g. /properties/id, /enum at registry-block-paths) — eliminates heuristic exclusion. The plan acknowledged the AST option as the rigor-foreclosing path but chose naive-now-rigorous-later — exactly the mandate-007 deferral.

evidence.reference: 'Naive recursive JSON walk + regex per Implementation step 3 is sufficient — pick complexity later only if false-positives surface' — verbatim mandate-007 deferral; the plan was approved and the test built per the naive form
the newly-landed citation-rot regression test (commit 4fd28a6) implements the naive form documented in the plan

impact: Two failure-mode classes unresolved by the naive gate. False-positive class: future legitimate canonical-id-vocabulary changes (e.g. shipping a registry of canonical_id form examples; expanding ID-format-illustration coverage) will trip the test and force either test-disablement OR ad-hoc exclusion accretion — both negative outcomes. False-negative class: citations that escape via multi-line strings or unusual formatting silently land in shipped artifacts post-FGAP-130 closure without the gate catching them; the gate's apparent green becomes a false-confidence signal. Both classes mean the FGAP-130 closure is not as robust as the VER-058 attestation implies. Combined with FGAP-131 (sibling-package scope gap), the overall citation-rot-foreclosure story is weaker than the merge message claims.
canonical_vocabulary: AST-based string-literal extraction; rigorous-gate vs heuristic-gate; mandate-007 deferred-fragility-foreclosure
proposed_resolution: CLOSED: implemented via FGAP-131/132 arc on branch fgap-131-132-rigorous-citation-gate (10 commits). The naive line-by-line CITATION_RE scan from FGAP-130 (commit 4fd28a6) has been deleted and replaced by packages/pi-context/src/citation-rot-scanner.ts — a rigorous AST + JSON + markdown/YAML body scanner. The six fragility classes the FGAP-132 body enumerated are each foreclosed: (1) multi-line strings — full-text scan with newline-counting line attribution catches multi-line straddle; (2) JSDoc / comment-exclusion ambiguity — TypeScript compiler API SyntaxKind discrimination ensures comment trivia is never visited by ts.forEachChild over CallExpression argument subtrees; (3) e.g.+NNN heuristic — heuristic removed entirely; format-illustration NNN-placeholders applied uniformly per Pattern C across all packages; (4) schema enum false-positive — JSON walker carves out enum string values inside *.schema.json explicitly; (5) path-suffix coarseness — JSONPath-aware carve-out narrows to top-level id under samples/blocks + registry/blocks + defaults/blocks + .project rather than whole-file suffix-exclusion; (6) JSDoc-vs-tool-description ambiguity — only string literals reachable through registerTool / Type.X CallExpression subtrees are flagged. 13/13 scanner unit tests pass including synthetic-injection probes per surface type. Attested by VER-059.
